title: Light Types and Scene Lighting

video.note: Covers point lights, intensity, source radius, temperature, light color, indirect lighting intensity, spotlights, inner and outer cone angles, rectangle lights, directional lights as sunlight, source angle, skylights, sky atmosphere, and Lumen settings in a post-process volume.

learningTarget: I can use different Unreal light types to create a clear mood and visual focus in a scene.

bellRinger.prompt: Where do you see different types of light in real life, and how do they change the mood of a space?

vocabulary.definition: A light that emits from one point in all directions, like a bulb.
A light that shines in one direction inside a cone shape.
A light that emits from a rectangular surface in one direction.
A light used for sun-like light from a far distance.
A light that captures the sky or distant scene and applies it to the world.
The strength or power of a light.
A warm or cool color quality of light.
A darker area created when light is blocked.
The feeling created by lighting, color, contrast, and focus.
The part of the scene the viewer should notice first.

slides.title: Lesson 14 - Light Types and Scene Lighting

assignment.title: Three-Light Mood Study
evidenceRequired: Google Drive link to a screenshot of setup using point light.
Google Drive link to a screenshot of setup using spotlight or rectangle light.
Google Drive link to a screenshot of setup using directional light, skylight, or environment lighting.
Short reflection explaining which setup created the strongest mood and why.

exitTicket: Which light type helped your scene most today, and what did it change visually?
